pattern: Two independent running tallies (a prefix XOR and a prefix odd-minus-even count) both need to repeat for a stretch between them to cancel out and stay balanced, so a single composite key over both tallies turns the search into a first-seen-index lookup.
hints: A subarray's XOR is zero exactly when the running XOR is the same at its start and its end, and its even/odd counts are equal exactly when a running (odd count minus even count) tally is also the same at both ends.
Track both running values together as one pair while scanning left to right, and remember, for every pair value you've seen, the earliest index at which it occurred.

Whenever the current pair repeats a pair you've already recorded, the distance back to that pair's *first* recorded index is a candidate answer -- keep the first occurrence (never overwrite it) so every repeat gives the longest possible gap.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, balanced subarray shorter than the whole array
statement example, the whole array is balanced
statement example, single element satisfies neither condition
duplicate even pair has xor zero but zero odd numbers, so it must not count
four equal odd values xor to zero across the whole run but contain no even numbers at all
balanced subarray sits strictly inside a longer, unbalanced array
single element at the maximum allowed value
two-element array with no balanced subarray at all
an all-even, all-zero run has xor zero everywhere but never has any odd numbers to balance against

stress.description: large random array across the full value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
